title: “Glassblowing Taster Experience – Make a Garden Gazing Ball, Bud Vase, or a Drinking Glass”
description: Come Experience the Magic of Glassblowing at The Curious Forge!, Have you ever wanted to try your hand at glassblowing? Our 30-minute taster sessions are your chance to experience this ancient and mesmerizing art form up close — and walk away with a stunning piece you made yourself!, Under the supervision and guidance of our professional glassblowers, you’ll discover the joy of molding hot molten glass into your very own unique creation. Choose from a colorful garden gazing ball, a drinking glass, or a bud vase — a beautiful keepsake for yourself or an unforgettable handmade gift for someone special., Bringing a friend, partner, or family? We’d love to have your whole group! Each session accommodates one participant at a time, we recommend booking consecutive time slots so everyone gets their turn at the torch. While one person is creating, the rest of the group gets a front-row seat to watch the magic unfold — it makes for a truly memorable shared experience!, A few things to know:Cost: $65 per person, Sessions are 30 minutes | One participant per session, Open to adults and teens age 14+, Preregistration is required — click on your preferred date and time slot to reserve your space, Your finished glass creation will be ready for pickup at The Forge within a few days, or you can have it shipped to you for a small shipping and handling fee

title: Lavender Glow | Queer Art & Our Shared History
description: Beginning with Nisenan history into the present day, Lavender Glow reflects upon and celebrates the lived experiences and legacies of queer individuals and groups whose collective influence demonstrate a profound investment in place, arts, and culture. Alongside highlights of local history, Lavender Glow features 44 artworks selected from 127 submissions.

title: Advanced Glassblowing Level 4 – Discovering New Techniques
description: Glassblowing Level 4– Advanced Glassblowing Techniques, Prerequisite: for those who have completed our level 3 Class at The Forge or at least 11 hours of glassblowing class instruction at The Forge., Continue your Glassblowing adventures in this 4-hour workshop. Work with Robert and learn advanced glassblowing techniques. You will also learn basic teamwork skills necessary to move forward to the next level of glassblowing classes., Learn how to use frit and powders to achieve patterns other then the spotted look we are familiar with. Create the look of feathers, scales, stone, cracked earth and other effects. Your instructor will spend the first hour and a half demonstrating them and students will be let loose to try some for themselves., Your stunning glass creations will be ready for pickup at The Forge within a few days of your class, or you can opt for the convenience of having your masterpiece shipped to you for a small shipping and handling fee.Ages 16+ and Adults, Class minimum 2 students/maximum 4 students.

title: Booker T. Jones
description: Booker T. Jones is a Rock & Roll Hall of Fame inductee, Grammy Lifetime Achievement Award recipient, and one of the key architects of the Stax Records sound. As the leader of Booker T. & the MGs, he helped shape modern soul music and created one of the most recognizable instrumental tracks of all time: “Green Onions.”, His influence reaches across decades, collaborating with legends such as Otis Redding, Sam & Dave, Wilson Pickett, Bob Dylan, Neil Young, and Willie Nelson. Jones remains an essential force in American music, touring internationally and continuing to inspire new generations with his unmistakable Hammond B3 mastery and timeless groove.

description: Interested in weaving but don't have the space or budget for a loom? Think again! In this beginner friendly workshop, you will make your own frame loom and learn how to weave on it.

title: Saturday Get Behind the Wheel Ceramics
description: Saturday Get Behind the Wheel Ceramics

title: Kid’s Creation Day! Silly Sculptures & Fun Figures in Polymer Clay
description: Kids’ Creation Day! Fun with Polymer Clay, Join us for a colorful, hands-on adventure in sculpting, shaping, and imagining! Polymer clay is the perfect, no-mess medium for kids of all ages to explore their creativity. In this fun and flexible workshop, young makers will experiment with different texturing tools, colors, and techniques to bring their ideas to life — from silly creatures and tiny treasures to action figurines and imaginative keepsakes., We’ll bake each creation during class so everyone goes home with a finished, cured piece they can proudly display. If time allows, we’ll even add a splash of color with paints for extra flair!, Our youth workshops are designed to be both playful and educational, guided by experienced instructors who love helping kids discover the joy of making. Every class is a new opportunity for your child to explore, experiment, and create something totally unique., Ages: 6-16 (no experience needed), Schedule: Offered once a month — 1-day workshop (1–2 hours), Cost: $30 + $15 materials fee, Class Size: Minimum 3 / Maximum 8 participants, Let your child’s imagination run wild — the sky’s the limit!

title: Arts & Crafts Summer Market
description: ARTS & CRAFTS SUMMER MARKET, SHOP • SIP • SUPPORT, DISCOVER HANDCRAFTED ART, GIFTS & SUMMER SHOPPING FUN, Hosted by the American Legion Post 130 & the Craft Guild of Nevada County.

title: Eli Rush Retrospective Art Show
Movies Under the Pines: School of Rock
description: This year, enjoy the laugh out loud, family friendly comedy School of Rock (2003) on the largest outdoor screen in Nevada County — a towering 32 feet of pure cinematic magic!, Before the film begins, come early and soak in the summer vibes with:, · Lawn games including Croquet, Cornhole, and more., · Community Record Sale & Air Guitar Contest, · Wear your favorite band shirt and be entered to win a free pass to the 26th Annual Nevada City Film Festival, Get ready to turn it up to eleven, because School of Rock is the ultimate feel-good film the whole family has been waiting for! Jack Black delivers an absolutely electric, laugh-out-loud performance as the hilariously over-the-top Dewey Finn — a wannabe rock god who accidentally becomes the best (and most unqualified) substitute teacher in history., Directed by the masterful Richard Linklater, this instant classic perfectly blends side-splitting comedy with an irresistible love letter to the golden age of hard rock that will have parents grinning ear-to-ear with nostalgia while their kids discover the power of the guitar solo for the very first time., Now in its 12th year, Movies Under the Pines transforms Pioneer Park into a state-of-the-art outdoor movie theater. Bring your blankets and low-back chairs and settle in for a truly special summer evening. Movies Under the Pines is proudly supported by the City of Nevada City., Beer, wine, popcorn, soft drinks, and water will be available for purchase. Outside food and non-alcoholic beverages are welcome; outside alcohol is not permitted., This event also marks the kick-off to the 26th Annual Nevada City Film Festival, happening June 26-28. The award-winning festival brings together filmmakers and movie lovers from around the world for three days of screenings, Q&As, kids programming, workshops, parties, and more. Tickets are on sale now.

title: Truckee Roundhouse Maker Show 2026
description: Join the Truckee Roundhouse for a celebration of art, community, and ingenuity on Sunday, June 7th for the 10th annual Maker Show! This event brings together our entire creative community – Artists, Inventors, Non-profits, Performers, and Sponsors to IGNITE THE IMAGINATION., This is not just a craft fair – at the Maker Show, YOU are the artist. Try sewing, wood turning, blacksmithing, candlemaking, screen printing, block printing and more. We'll have a variety of interactive arts, technologies, creative workshops, a maker market, food trucks, beer, music, art cars, kids activities, a silent auction, and raffle prizes., Buy your tickets in advance to save $5!

title: Ceramics 101 – Mud Therapy
description: Discover the joy of working with clay in this immersive 3 class session workshop at The Curious Forge! Whether you’re brand new to ceramics or looking to expand your skills, this class with instructor Bob Davis is designed to give you a well-rounded understanding of this dynamic medium.

title: The Creative Labyrinth: Nature & Creativity Bookmaking
description: This immersive workshop invites participants into bookmaking as a creative and reflective journey., Using creative folded book structures, natural materials, and guided creativity practices, participants are introduced to a process that blends hands-on making with personal exploration. Each book becomes more than an object—it becomes a container for thought, intuition, and meaning., Rather than focusing on traditional bookbinding techniques, this workshop emphasizes accessible folded forms that allow for both completion and creative freedom. Participants will create three unique handmade books while exploring a deeper connection to their own creative voice.

title: The Good, the Bad, and the Ugly (Il Buono, il brutto, il cattivo)
description: 6/7 - Considered some of the greatest Westerns of all time, Sergio Leone’s A Man with No Name trilogy forever popularized the spaghetti Western genre, proved highly influential to everything that came after, and launched Clint Eastwood to stardom. The Onyx Downtown at the Nevada Theatre is proud to present all three films in the acclaimed trilogy, restored in beautiful 4K! A partnership between The Man with No Name and Tuco (Eli Wallach) goes awry, only for fate to intervene in the form of information about a cache of stolen Confederate gold buried in a graveyard. Each possessing a different clue to its location, the pair are forced into a distrustful partnership. However, the gold is also sought by Angel Eyes (Lee Van Cleef), a ruthless mercenary with his own twisted code of honor.

title: Matt Stone as ELVIS
description: Matt Stone as Elvis is an internationally recognized concert experience that delivers the closest thing to seeing Elvis Presley live., Endorsed by Graceland and the Elvis Presley Estate, Matt Stone is celebrated for his extraordinary vocal accuracy, stage presence, and attention to detail. This is not a parody or impersonation — it’s a full-scale theatrical concert portraying Elvis at the peak of his power., Backed by a sensational live band, Elvis: In Person recreates the energy, charisma, and sound that made Elvis the greatest entertainer of all time. Fans consistently leave believing they’ve just witnessed the King himself.

description: Come join our vibrant African Drum and Dance classes! Open to all ages and abilities, we explore different styles from across the African Diasporic disciplines. Including Malian, Congolese, Guinean, Senegalese, Afro-Cuban and Afro-Brazilian. Masters of their craft travel from all over to share their culture and heritage with our beautiful community, along with our own fabulous local teachers and percussionists, to offer a truly inspiring and engaging experience

title: Intro to Stained Glass – Level 1: Straight Cuts
description: Experience the art of stained glass in this beginner-friendly workshop! Join us to craft a small suncatcher using vibrant glass pieces in this class with Charne Morris. No experience necessary—just bring your enthusiasm!, This introductory class focuses on straight cuts and will teach you the basics of stained glass safety., Throughout this 3-hour class, you will learn pattern transfer, glass cutting, grinding, foiling, soldering, and polishing. You will go home with a finished project piece that is roughly 3.5” x 4”., A materials cost of $20 is required on the day. Payment to the facilitator can be made using cash or Venmo., What You’ll Learn:, Basics of stained glass art, How glass behaves and the creation process, Selecting and cutting glass pieces, Creating a stained glass suncatcher using the copper foil method and soldering techniques, Details:, Duration: 3 hours, Price: $79 class fee + plus additional material fee $20 is required during class. Payment to the facilitator can be made using cash or Venmo., Ages: Adults and 16+, Participants: Minimum of 3, maximum of 6

title: Eli Rush Retrospective Art Show

THURSDAY NIGHT MARKET SUMMER SERIES ─ Grass Valley’s Favorite Summer Tradition is Back! Warm evenings, historic charm, and the buzz of community life—welcome to the Thursday Night Market Summer Series in Downtown Grass Valley!
description: Warm evenings, historic charm, and the buzz of community life—welcome to the Thursday Night Market Summer Series in Downtown Grass Valley! Every Thursday evening, our charming streets come alive with an eclectic mix of artisan vendors, farm-fresh produce, sizzling street food, and toe-tapping live music., Wander through local maker booths, sip something cool, and catch up with friends old and new. From handmade crafts and gourmet treats to the farm fresh produce at the Certified Farmers' Market and family fun, there’s something for everyone. Whether you’re a long-time local or a first-time visitor, this is summer in Grass Valley at its best—vibrant, friendly, and unforgettable.

title: Eli Rush Retrospective Art Show
From the Hip | Casual Photos of Contradictions & Conundrums
description: We live in a world where inconsistency, paradox and irony flourishes while commodities attempt to trump community. We’re inundated by photographs of products and services for sale, many are clever, but lack soul. Meanwhile, almost everyone has a camera on their phone and photography is immensely popular. It can be rewarding – there are many campfires tended to by the positive and playful. Expressions of this impulse are portrayed in photographs that provide another point of view, a smile, a good laugh or even an insight., Photographer and experimental geographer, Hank Meals, has stalked and stumbled into off-handed imagery for years and he’s assembled a collection in his latest slide show From the Hip: Casual Photos of Contradictions and Conundrums., Hank will provide ongoing commentary informed by his background in anthropology, history, geography, art and a simple version of the good life. Here’s an opportunity to expand your views while increasing your pleasure and perception with this entertaining and informative presentation.

title: Lucinda Williams
description: Lucinda Williams brings her band to the Marisa Funk Theater at The Center for the Arts in Grass Valley on June 12. The New York Times Magazine just named her one of the 30 greatest American singer-songwriters — and she's arriving with a brand new album to prove it. World's Gone Wrong, her eighteenth studio album, was written and recorded in a matter of weeks this spring out of pure urgency — she set aside a nearly finished record to make it. Forty-five years in, three Grammys, and Car Wheels on a Gravel Road in her catalog, Williams is still making music that feels like it had to exist. This is not a nostalgia tour. This is an artist with something to say.

title: Watercolor Exploration with Chappy Valente
description: Exploring Watercolor with Chappy Valente, Description:, Immerse yourself in the expressive world of watercolor painting! Whether you’re brand new to watercolor or looking to expand your skills, this hands-on class with artist and educator Chappy Valente will help you gain confidence and technique. Learn to paint with fluidity and intention as you explore the tools, papers, and brushwork that make watercolor such a luminous and rewarding medium., Each session is a stand-alone class with fresh projects and techniques—join once or come monthly to continue growing your watercolor practice., What’s the Project:, Create your own watercolor studies exploring washes, color mixing, and texture effects. Each class introduces a new focus—such as landscapes, botanicals, or abstract forms—to inspire your creativity., What Will They Learn:, Brush control and paint-to-water ratios, Wash techniques (wet-on-wet, wet-on-dry, gradient), Paper types, brush styles, and pigment selection, Layering, and color blending, Tips for composition and adding expressive detail, Materials Needed:, All materials are provided for in-class use. Students are welcome to bring their own favorite brushes or watercolor set if desired., Skill Level / Prerequisites:, All skill levels welcome—from first-time painters to those continuing their watercolor journey., Class Length:, One 3-hour session, (Optional continuing classes offered monthly), Fee: $50 + $20 materials, Minimum 3 / Maximum 6 participants

title: Eli Rush Retrospective Art Show
The Floyd - A Tribute to Pink Floyd
description: Experience the music and atmosphere of Pink Floyd live on stage when The Floyd Nevada comes to the Main Stage - Marisa Funk Theater on Saturday, June 13., Dedicated to recreating the sound, mood, and sonic depth of Pink Floyd’s most iconic eras, The Floyd Nevada delivers soaring guitar solos, expansive instrumentals, and immersive lighting that transports audiences back to the golden age of progressive rock. From atmospheric ballads to electrifying anthems, this tribute honors one of rock’s most influential bands with precision and passion., Saturday, June 13, 2026, Doors 7:00 PM | Show 8:00 PM

title: The Creative Labyrinth: Nature & Creativity Bookmaking
description: This immersive workshop invites participants into bookmaking as a creative and reflective journey., Using creative folded book structures, natural materials, and guided creativity practices, participants are introduced to a process that blends hands-on making with personal exploration. Each book becomes more than an object—it becomes a container for thought, intuition, and meaning., Rather than focusing on traditional bookbinding techniques, this workshop emphasizes accessible folded forms that allow for both completion and creative freedom. Participants will create three unique handmade books while exploring a deeper connection to their own creative voice.

description: Free Jazz Picnic in the Park.

title: Word Jam
description: Join the Word Jam that takes place at 7pm on the second and fourth Sundays. Participants get 5 minutes to read a piece they have written

title: But I'm a Cheerleader
description: 6/14 - The Onyx Downtown is incredibly proud to once again partner with Nevada County Pride for this special, one-night-only screening at the Nevada Theatre of the cult classic BUT I’M A CHEERLEADER! In this classic satire directed by Jamie Babbit, parents who are worried that their children might not be walking the straight and narrow path discover a rehabilitation camp designed to curb alternative lifestyles. Megan (Natasha Lyonne), a high school student and member of the cheerleading squad, seems like an ordinary enough teenage girl, but her habit of honestly expressing herself and lack of romantic enthusiasm for her boyfriend convince her very repressed parents that Megan is becoming a lesbian. So Megan is shipped off to True Directions, a camp for gay and gay-leaning teens, where Mary Brown (Cathy Moriarty) and Mike (RuPaul) attempt to deprogram kids with homosexual tendencies.

title: Sierra Jazz Cammp
description: Jazz Camp is a fun, focused, and supportive environment. You’ll jam with motivated peers and study with a faculty of professional musicians and educators. This camp is for advanced middle school musicians through adults. Its for instrumentalists and vocalists. The vibe is relaxed, fun and the venue is a peaceful country setting outside Nevada City, CA.

title: Digital Arts Exploration – Youth Maker XD Camp Experience
description: Camp participants will be designing and creating an LED Laser Cut projects using both 3d and 2d items from leather, wood, MDF, acrylic, and or fabric. Discover the art of 3D printing on our new Bambu 3D printers. There will be a thorough discussion on how the machines work, how to operate them safely, finding design sources, and machine constraints.

title: Sierra Jazz Camp

title: Ceramics Hand-Building Adventures Youth Weekly Workshop Series
description: Come play with clay!, In this fun and interactive class, students will create ceramic pottery using clay and their imagination! Handbuilding allows participants to learn how to press decorative designs into clay for unique one of kind pottery pieces. Student will learn to to roll clay on the slab roller, design, cut, and decorate their piece with a variety of textures. Pieces will be bisque fired and then the glazing fun begins., You will be notified to pick you your child’s ceramic pieces after they have been glaze fired, approximately 10 days after the last class., Our classes are designed to be both fun and educational, with experienced instructors who are passionate about sharing their skills and knowledge with young people. We take great care to create a welcoming and inclusive environment where every child can feel valued and supported., Ages 8 to 17, 4 class series – each Monday weekly, $160, Class 4 minimum/6 maximum participants

title: Spring Street Swing Out
venue_name: Odd Fellows Hall, 212 Spring Street, Nevada City CA 95959
description: 🎶 Get Ready to Swing the Night Away! 💃🕺, Join us for our monthly Swing Dance & Lindy Hop Night – a high-energy evening filled with music, movement, and community fun!, 🕕 Intro Lesson: 6–7 PM – Perfect for beginners! No experience or partner needed., 🎉 Social Dance to follow – Dance the night away with fellow swing lovers!, 💵 Tickets:, • $10 – DJ Nights (6pm-9pm), • $15 – Live Band Nights (6pm-10pm), 🎓 Students: $5 off with valid ID!, Beer and Nevada City Winery wine available for purchase., 🎷Check our calendar for upcoming live band nights and special events:, 👉 nevadacountyswings.org, Whether you're stepping onto the dance floor for the first time or you're a Lindy pro, there's a spot for you. See you there!

title: Asleep at the Wheel
description: Western swing legends Asleep at the Wheel bring their timeless sound to The Center for the Arts on June 16., With over five decades of Grammy-winning music, the band has become synonymous with high-energy performances, masterful musicianship, and dance-floor-ready classics., Tickets go on sale April 17 at 10am., Don’t miss this feel-good night of country, jazz, and swing—all in one unforgettable performance.

title: Lavender Glow | Queer Art & Our Shared History
description: Beginning with Nisenan history into the present day, Lavender Glow reflects upon and celebrates the lived experiences and legacies of queer individuals and groups whose collective influence demonstrate a profound investment in place, arts, and culture. Alongside highlights of local history, Lavender Glow features 44 artworks selected from 127 submissions.

title: Digital Arts Exploration – Youth Maker XD Camp Experience
description: Camp participants will be designing and creating an LED Laser Cut projects using both 3d and 2d items from leather, wood, MDF, acrylic, and or fabric. Discover the art of 3D printing on our new Bambu 3D printers. There will be a thorough discussion on how the machines work, how to operate them safely, finding design sources, and machine constraints.

title: Wonder Circle Mythic Art & Nature Journey Youth Maker XD Camp Experience
description: Some children see the world a little differently — they notice the light through the leaves, the shape of a shadow, the story hiding inside a stone. The Wonder Circle was made for them.

title: Playwriting Group
description: Sent upon request. (We meet in each other's homes.) All playwrights welcome! Whether playwriting is new to you or you're already an accomplished playwright, come join this on-going bi-weekly writers' group. We'll spend our time focusing on our writing, but with that magical element of doing so within the presence of others. Come discover the amazing experience of writing within the context of a group. Bring your computer, notebook, iPad, or whatever you like to use for writing. We share resources, discuss our goals and challenges, even provide feedback, but the primary focus of the group is to work on your own play! We'll do a check-in/discussion at the beginning of the meeting, then write in silence, and check-out at the end of our session. Contact Kimberly for the location and to answer any questions you may have.

title: Eli Rush Retrospective Art Show

title: THURSDAY NIGHT MARKET SUMMER SERIES ─ Grass Valley’s Favorite Summer Tradition is Back! Warm evenings, historic charm, and the buzz of community life—welcome to the Thursday Night Market Summer Series in Downtown Grass Valley!
description: Warm evenings, historic charm, and the buzz of community life—welcome to the Thursday Night Market Summer Series in Downtown Grass Valley! Every Thursday evening, our charming streets come alive with an eclectic mix of artisan vendors, farm-fresh produce, sizzling street food, and toe-tapping live music., Wander through local maker booths, sip something cool, and catch up with friends old and new. From handmade crafts and gourmet treats to the farm fresh produce at the Certified Farmers' Market and family fun, there’s something for everyone. Whether you’re a long-time local or a first-time visitor, this is summer in Grass Valley at its best—vibrant, friendly, and unforgettable.
